conditionDescriptions: Chronic kidney disease with creatinine clearance less than 30 mL/min
contraindications.clinicalRationale: Reduced renal clearance in severe CKD may lead to accumulation of perindopril and its metabolites, requiring dose adjustment or alternative therapy. While perindopril and metabolites can be removed by hemodialysis, use is not recommended when CrCl <30 ml/min.
contraindications.riskDescription: Risk of drug accumulation and potential adverse effects due to reduced renal clearance
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Hemodialysis can remove perindopril and its metabolites if dialysis is already indicated
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Perindopril: Use is not recommended when CrCl <30 ml/min. Perindopril and its metabolites are removed by hemodialysis.
